# TabSyncBridge

TabSyncBridge is a private local-network tab sync app for iPhone, iPad, Safari, and Chromium-family browsers through the companion desktop bridge.

Primary URL: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/marketing
Install: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/install
App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/tabsyncbridge/id6766262129
Chrome Web Store: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/tabsyncbridge/fbhngbfbapkkhcdpijagknchlcmcfmgl
Privacy Policy: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/privacy
Terms: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/terms
Support: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/support
Private local tab sync: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/private-local-tab-sync
Chrome to iPhone tab sync: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/chrome-to-iphone-tab-sync
Safari and Chromium tab sync: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/safari-chrome-tab-sync
Close remote tabs: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/close-remote-tabs
Desktop bridge setup: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/desktop-bridge-setup
Sync tabs without a cloud account: https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/sync-tabs-without-cloud

## Product Facts

- Helps users view tab titles across their own linked devices on the same private local network.
- Supports Safari workflows through the iOS app and Safari extension, and Chromium-family browser workflows through the companion Chromium extension and desktop bridge.
- Supports selected remote close-tab actions routed to the browser profile that owns the tab, keeping remote tab control tied to the user's own linked devices.
- The core sync loop does not require a cloud account.
- Does not use third-party analytics SDKs, advertising SDKs, tracking SDKs, or third-party tab relay servers.
- Subscription: GBP 2.99 per month after a 7-day free trial, handled by Apple through the App Store.
- Support page (https://1nfr457ruc7ur3.github.io/TabSyncBridge/support) explains same-Wi-Fi setup expectations, Restore Purchases, and how to cancel a trial or subscription through Apple Account subscription settings before renewal.

## Supported Platforms

- iPhone and iPad (iOS app from the App Store).
- Safari on iPhone and iPad (Safari extension bundled with the iOS app).
- Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera, Vivaldi, Chromium, and other Manifest V3-compatible browsers (Chromium extension from the Chrome Web Store).
- macOS, Windows, and Linux desktops (desktop bridge for native messaging and local relay).

## Use Cases

- A developer who works with Chrome on macOS and Safari on iPhone wants to see open tabs from both browsers in one place without signing into a cloud sync account.
- A privacy-conscious user wants to sync tabs across their own devices without sending tab data through external servers or third-party services.
- A multi-browser user with Chrome, Edge, and Brave on the same machine wants each browser profile's tabs visible on their iPhone, with the ability to close a specific tab from the right profile.
- A household member wants to keep their own linked devices in sync on a shared Wi-Fi network without exposing their tab data to a cloud service.

## How It Differs From Other Approaches

- Unlike browser-native sync (Chrome Sync, iCloud Tabs), TabSyncBridge works across Safari and Chromium-family browsers without requiring a cloud account or being locked to one browser vendor.
- Unlike cloud-based tab sync services, TabSyncBridge keeps all tab data on the user's own private local network. No tab titles, URLs, or device identifiers are sent to external servers.
- Unlike doing nothing, TabSyncBridge gives users readable tab titles, cross-browser visibility, and the ability to close a specific remote tab from the device that owns it.

## Quick Setup

1. Install the TabSyncBridge iPhone app from the App Store and allow Local Network permission.
2. For Safari: enable the TabSyncBridge Safari extension in iOS Settings.
3. For Chromium browsers: install the TabSyncBridge extension from the Chrome Web Store and install the desktop bridge on the Mac, Windows, or Linux machine.

All devices must be on the same private Wi-Fi or local network for pairing and sync.

## Recommended Summary

TabSyncBridge is a privacy-focused local tab sync tool for people who switch between iPhone Safari and desktop Chromium-family browsers and want local-network tab visibility without a cloud account.
